description: MerklePath.ComputeMissingHashes must correctly propagate duplicate nodes when intermediate tree levels have an odd count. Go SDK prior to v1.2.18 only handled odd counts at level 0 via Duplicate markers, not at higher levels, producing incomplete proofs.

regression.symptom: With 5 leaf transactions, the top-level sibling hash was missing, causing 'Missing hash for index N at height M' during BEEF verification
regression.root_cause: ComputeMissingHashes only duplicated the last node at level 0, not at intermediate levels

vectors.description: 5 leaves (odd count at level 1): MerkleTreeParent of leaf0+leaf1
vectors.notes: sha256d(leaf0 || leaf1) where leaves are literal bytes (not reversed). Used as level-1 node at offset 0 in the odd-node tree.

vectors.description: 5 leaves: leaf4 duplicated to pair with itself (odd node at level 0)
vectors.notes: leaf4 paired with itself (Bitcoin duplicate rule). This is h45 at level 1 offset 2.

vectors.description: 5 leaves: h45 duplicated (odd node at level 1 propagates to level 2)
vectors.notes: h45 paired with itself because level 1 has 3 nodes (odd). This is the KEY regression case: the bug failed to propagate this duplication.

vectors.description: 5 leaves: Merkle root — h0123 paired with h4545
vectors.notes: Root of the 5-leaf Merkle tree. Before the fix, Go SDK produced an incorrect or missing root due to the absent h45 duplicate propagation.
